item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: There's no indication these images were released under Creative Commons. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry it took me a while to respond, I'd like to hear all of your critiques. I kind of fell out of the SCP thing but I'm starting to get back into it. Yeah, please send me all I should change, I'm looking to fix it soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has a lot of minor stylistic errors - grammar snarls, incorrect terminology, confused and ambiguous descriptions, and straight up mistakes. This feels like it could have really done with a good sweep for nitpicks at the draft stage.

There's also some style problems - the description of the actual mechanics is hazy and confusing, and the scare-quote witness descriptions of the box are unprofessional. I'm not going to do that here, but let me know if you'd like me to do a full breakdown if you're planning on doing an edit sweep.

item.title: Thanks
item.content:encoded: I went and omitted the redundant and unnecessary details of "a sort of" and "it is important to state." If you have any ideas of how to fix the addendum, please let me know through a message. Thanks for leaving feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this SCP! It's a very interesting and fun concept, however I have some nitpicks. The phrase "it is important to state" before one of the paragraphs is redundant, since it should be assumed everything in the article is important to the reader (why would they have superfluous details?). Also, the phrase (a sort of seed) sounds to inconclusive of the Foundation. More concrete or open phrasing, such as "SCP-3603 instances act as a seed when planted" , or just "SCP-3603 instances convert into a unique seed". Also, the addendum feels too loose; the notes sound too unprofessional, and the descriptions of the effects do too. HOWEVER, this SCP has a lot of potential, and with more tuning and buffing this can be a truly damn good SCP. +1

item.title: Pics
item.content:encoded:
So I forgot to post where I got the pics from! I really am dumb. Here are the two images:
Captain Crunch: http://www.upcitemdb.com/upc/30000311684
Blue-looking black bear: http://www.petworlds.net/american-black-bear/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can respect that. What could I do better in the future?
Edit: NVM I'm an idiot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All Bearies. Ha.

although several people have stated "It just seems, off" or "I don't like the way the Captain is looking at me."

SCP-3603 Colors: Green and Blue
Powder Color: Turquoise
Resulting SCP-3603-1 Anomaly: [REDACTED]
Notes: Please create no more. Do not create any more. Not after what it did to Johnson. No more.

These lines in particular are keeping me at a novote. I've never been a fan of direct 'descriptive quotes' in the object description. It doesn't read right to me.

My problem with the turquoise test is mostly with the end note. It's a pretty hammy and clumsy way to say 'Hey, look. Something bad happened here.'

Beyond that, the idea itself is… pretty neat and pretty simple. I kind of like it, but issues like the above just hold it back for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even the cereal got fucked by bears, what is this world coming to?
